{"query":"Ursula von der Leyen July 2025 EU US trade agreement 15% tariffs European exports details official","model":"mako","response_time_ms":2093,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://commission.europa.eu/topics/trade/eu-us-trade-deal_en","title":"The EU-US trade deal - European Commission","content":"Following the formal approval by the European Parliament and Council of the EU, the EU eliminated all duties on imports of US industrial goods and improved market access for certain non-sensitive agri-food products as from 1 July 2026.
US tariff ceiling of 15% for most EU exports
This special MFN-tariff-only regime will apply to unavailable natural resources (such as cork), all aircraft and aircraft parts, generic pharmaceuticals and their ingredients and chemical precursors.
A joint effort to protect the steel and aluminium sectors from unfair and distortive competition."},{"url":"https://www.whitehouse.gov/fact-sheets/2025/07/fact-sheet-the-united-states-and-european-union-reach-massive-trade-deal/","title":"Fact Sheet: The United States and European Union Reach Massive Trade Deal – The White House","content":"The EU will purchase $750 billion in U.S. energy and make new investments of $600 billion in the United States, all by 2028.
As part of President Trump’s strategy to establish balanced trade, the European Union will pay the United States a tariff rate of 15%, including on autos and auto parts, pharmaceuticals, and semiconductors.
The two sides will take complementary actions to address non-market policies of third parties, as well as cooperating on inbound and outbound investment reviews, export controls, and duty evasion.
Commercial Deals: The United States and European Union recognize a series of major commercial agreements across key sectors—including energy and semiconductors—that will further expand U.S. exports to the European market.
In just six short months, President Trump has reaffirmed the United States as the world’s most attractive destination for investment and the unrivaled leader in innovation, research, and advanced manufacturing."},{"url":"https://www.iwkoeln.de/en/studies/samina-sultan-trumps-tariffs-a-deal-is-a-deal.html","title":"Trump’s tariffs: A deal is a deal? - German Economic Institute (IW)","content":"Imports from Canada and Mexico, in contrast, were effectively only tariffed at 3.8 per cent.
The focus is a deeper analysis of the effects of the US–EU deal, the so-called Turnberry Deal, effective from September 2025 to February 2026.
For both sectors the EU’s effective tariff rates were much higher than for Southeast Asian competitors like Taiwan, Vietnam, or South Korea.
China and India but also South Korea and Vietnam were the main beneficiaries of this new temporary tariff structure, because their effective tariff rates were reduced considerably.
For instance, China’s effective tariff rate was reduced by almost 9 per centage points from February 2026 to 23.1 per cent in March 2026."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/statement-president-von-der-leyen-deal-tariffs-trade-von-der-leyen-wm0he","title":"Statement by President von der Leyen on the deal on tariffs and trade with the United States","content":"Allow me to go into some details.
This rate applies across most sectors, including cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals.
This 15% is a clear ceiling.
Madam President, Madam Minister, Honourable Members, Indeed, we discussed today EU-China relations ahead of the China…"},{"url":"https://www.hsfkramer.com/notes/crt/2025-09/deal","title":null,"content":"The deal reached between Commission president Ursula von der Leyen and US president Trump in Turnberry, Scotland on 27 July 2025 has been published by the European Commission in a Joint Statement of 21 August 2025.
US automobile tariffs under Section 232 of the US Trade Expansion Act will also be limited to 15%.
The US also promises to limit the tariff that it applies to EU goods in sectors where Section 232 tariffs are pending (pharmaceuticals, semiconductors and lumber) to 15%."}]}

{"query":"Ursula von der Leyen July 2025 EU US trade agreement 15% tariffs European exports details official","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KVWxb22wSgo","title":"What's in the EU-US trade deal: Von der Leyen's full remarks | DW News","content":"What's in the EU-US trade deal: Von der Leyen's full remarks | DW News
DW News
6340000 subscribers
426 likes
29029 views
28 Jul 2025
US President Donald Trump and the European Union chief Ursula von der Leyen have reached a trade deal during talks in Scotland. 
The agreement caps months of negotiations. Most tariffs on exports from the EU will be set at 15%, including for cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals. A US official said that Washington would keep its 50 percent tariff on steel and aluminium imports. Trump says the EU will invest an extra 600 Bbillion dollars in America, and buy 750 Bbillion dollars worth of US energy.
DW's Teru Schulz and Steven Beardley provide analysis.

Under the agreed terms, finalised on Sunday by European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen and US President Donald Trump during a meeting in Scotland, the majority of EU exports bound for the American market will be subject to a 15% tariff. "I think it's great we made a deal today instead of playing games," Trump said at the end of the meeting. It's a huge deal," von der Leyen said.
